title: Decomposition eval annotation
description: Canonical contract for decomposition quality annotations. A document validates as either the logical annotation shape (repo/runtime objects) or the Phoenix wire shape (REST annotation payload entries, which nest label/score/explanation under result and add trace_id/span_id). Both shapes require a non-empty identifier because Phoenix upserts by (name, target, identifier). Human, model, and code signals share one failure taxonomy; human and model judges share the quality rubric in rubrics/decomposition-quality.md. Controller promotion outcome annotations use the separate minimal $defs/promotion_outcome_annotation contract, not this oneOf.

annotation_name.description: Canonical decomposition annotation names. Each dimension is a separate annotation name. decomposition_quality is the roll-up used for default gates and must not drift. accepted_packet_sufficiency and pause_state_correctness are deterministic-check names stored with annotator_kind CODE.

annotator_kind.description: CODE is only for deterministic-check storage. Deterministic checks stored as CODE are a wire format detail, not a third peer judge, and must never be spoofed as HUMAN or LLM.

quality_label.description: Default-judge label set: pass | needs_revision | blocking_failure. This set must not drift. Default score bands for HUMAN and LLM quality annotations: pass 0.80-1.00, needs_revision 0.40-0.79, blocking_failure 0.00-0.39 (or any critical failure mode that invalidates the output). Bands are documented defaults, not hard validation: a label/score band mismatch is a low-confidence worklist flag, not a schema rejection, and CODE deterministic checks may use binary scores (0 or 1) for structural invariants.

quality_score.description: Quality score in [0, 1]. See quality_label for default per-label score bands; see pass_band/needs_revision_band/blocking_failure_band for the machine-readable band definitions.

pass_band.description: Default score band for the pass label (HUMAN/LLM quality annotations).

needs_revision_band.description: Default score band for the needs_revision label (HUMAN/LLM quality annotations).

blocking_failure_band.description: Default score band for the blocking_failure label (HUMAN/LLM quality annotations). A blocking_failure may carry a score above this band when a critical failure mode invalidates the output.

no_workflow_state_keys.description: Denylist for Phoenix-bound metadata: no Agentic Factory workflow/queue state may be stored in Phoenix (wipe test: losing Phoenix must never lose which behavior is accepted or what queue is pending). These known banned keys are schema-level rejections; other extensibility stays open.

annotation_metadata.description: Shared annotation metadata. rubric_version and failure_taxonomy_version are required for HUMAN and LLM quality annotations (enforced by the parent shapes); CODE deterministic checks should carry them once wired into Phoenix-compatible storage. failure_mode_details preserves raw parameterized CODE diagnostics (<base_id>:<param>) whose base ids appear in failure_modes. Workflow/queue state keys are banned (see no_workflow_state_keys).

failure_modes.description: Failure mode ids drawn from the versioned taxonomy in failure-taxonomy.json. CODE emissions are normalized to taxonomy base ids (parameter detail moves to failure_mode_details).

failure_mode_details.description: Raw parameterized CODE diagnostics, for example missing_context_digest:<phase>. Each entry's base id (before the first colon) must appear in failure_modes.

identifier.description: Human/user id, judge id, or code evaluator id. Required and non-empty for canonical annotations: Phoenix upserts by (name, target, identifier) and defaults the identifier to the empty string, so an omitted or empty identifier can overwrite or merge human/model/code judgments that share a name and target. In MVP, human identifiers are asserted, not authenticated, because local Phoenix is unauthenticated.

human_or_llm_metadata_requirement.description: HUMAN and LLM quality annotations must record the rubric and failure taxonomy versions they were judged against.

phoenix_wire_annotation.title: Phoenix wire annotation shape
phoenix_wire_annotation.description: One entry of the data array sent to the Phoenix REST annotation endpoints. trace_id targets trace annotations; span_id targets span annotations only. At least one target id is required. Quality annotations must carry result.label, result.score, and result.explanation; CODE deterministic checks may use binary scores (0 or 1) but still report a score and an explanation.

promotion_outcome_annotation.title: Promotion outcome annotation (controller outcomes)
promotion_outcome_annotation.description: Separate minimal contract for the promotion controller's Phoenix outcome annotations. These record only actual controller outcomes and are observability, never authority: no queue or workflow state, no caller-granted routing. They are deliberately NOT part of the root oneOf (which is the quality contract); validate controller outcome payloads against this $def directly. Label-only by design - outcome annotations carry no quality score.

label.description: Actual controller outcome only. route_to_hitl means a HITL PR was proposed; blocked and superseded are controller outcomes, never caller-granted authority.

repo_review_url.description: HITL PR URL; null when the outcome produced no repo review artifact (for example blocked).

repair_state.description: Durable repair marker, for example none or phoenix_audit_retry_needed.
